---
title: "Case Studies"
subtitle: "Simulated epidemic data for cross-disciplinary analysis"
---

## Overview

The workshop is structured around two case studies derived from a single simulated population-wide outbreak. The underlying data are inspired by the SARS-CoV-2 pandemic and generated from a structured compartmental SEIRD model in a closed population of 1,000,000 individuals, with heterogeneity in susceptibility and infectiousness, an intervention period, and a vaccination campaign introduced partway through the outbreak.

::: {.callout-important}
Participants do **not** see the underlying complete individual-level dataset until the very end of the workshop. The two case study datasets are extracts designed to mimic realistic data availability settings.
:::

---

## Case Study Documents

```{=html}
<div class="doc-cards">
  <a href="assets/case-study-a.docx" class="doc-card" download>
    <img src="assets/thumbnails/case-study-a.png" alt="Case Study A" onerror="this.src='assets/thumbnails/placeholder.svg'">
    <div class="doc-card-label">Case Study A<br>Natural epidemic course</div>
  </a>
  <a href="assets/case-study-b.docx" class="doc-card" download>
    <img src="assets/thumbnails/case-study-b.png" alt="Case Study B" onerror="this.src='assets/thumbnails/placeholder.svg'">
    <div class="doc-card-label">Case Study B<br>Vaccine efficacy</div>
  </a>
</div>
```

---

## Case Study A — Natural Course of the Epidemic

**Focus period:** Before vaccination becomes available.

**Available data:** Daily aggregate counts of new infections (subject to reporting delays and underreporting), hospitalizations, and deaths.

**Analytical aims:**

- Estimate the reproduction number (R~0~ and R~eff~) over time
- Estimate the effect of intervention measures on transmission
- Predict the future course of the epidemic under different scenarios
- Handle incomplete data — censoring, reporting delays, and underreporting

---

## Case Study B — Vaccine Efficacy

**Focus period:** After vaccination becomes available.

**Available data:** Daily aggregate counts of infections, hospitalizations, deaths, and vaccinations (including uptake aggregated over population subgroups).

**Analytical aims:**

- Estimate vaccine efficacy (VE) against infection, hospitalization, and death
- Understand time-dependent vaccine effectiveness (waning immunity)
- Characterize heterogeneity in vaccine uptake and efficacy across subgroups

---

## Working Groups

Participants are divided into **four mixed-discipline groups** (two per case study), intentionally composed across SA and IDM expertise and career stage. Groups working on the same case study meet on Day 2 to compare analysis plans before refining independently. All groups share findings in the daily plenary sessions.
